compiled_state.title: As a developer, I can verify consumer discovery works end-to-end using 8df3-61d0 as a smoke test
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Description

**What**: Create a smoke test that runs the brainstorm scrutiny pipeline on an epic with the same description as closed epic 8df3-61d0 (Behavioral testing standard), and verifies that Part C enumerates 7+ files referencing behavioral-testing-standard.md and the fidelity reviewer flags any consumers not covered by the epic's success criteria.

**Why**: The 8df3-61d0 epic is the motivating example for this entire feature — it shipped with 4 agents covered when 7+ participate in the test quality lifecycle. This smoke test validates that the new consumer discovery mechanism would have caught that gap, providing confidence that the implementation works end-to-end.

**Scope**:
- IN: Creating a test that uses 8df3-61d0's description as input to the scrutiny pipeline; verifying Part C enumerates 7+ files referencing behavioral-testing-standard.md; verifying the fidelity reviewer flags consumers not covered by success criteria
- OUT: Modifying the scrutiny pipeline logic (covered by stories 1 and 2); testing against other epics or artifacts

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, running the smoke test with 8df3-61d0's description as input causes Part C to enumerate 7+ files referencing behavioral-testing-standard.md and the fidelity reviewer flags any not covered by the epic's success criteria
  <- Satisfies: "Smoke test: running brainstorm on an epic with the same description as closed epic 8df3-61d0, Part C enumerates 7+ files referencing behavioral-testing-standard.md and the fidelity reviewer flags any not covered by SCs"
- Unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Testing] The smoke test should mock the sub-agent dispatch for the fidelity reviewer while exercising real Part C scan logic against the actual codebase
- [Reliability] The exact count of files referencing behavioral-testing-standard.md may change as the codebase evolves — assert >= 7 rather than an exact count
